description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

v1.37.0

We've combined the heat index bar, rainfall bar, temperature graph, and lunar phase tracker into a single "Weather Chart". The moon marker now appears at the top of the chart at the transit time (southernmost point in the sky), so you can see at a glance when it will be visible in the sky.

• Integrated Weather Chart — condensed into one 160px-tall image. Background color shows the heat index (green → yellow → orange → red → dark red gradient), blue shading in the lower half shows rainfall probability, a darker blue bar in the center shows hourly rainfall amount, a thin curve shows temperature, and a dark overlay marks nighttime
• Moon marker at chart top — moon emoji positioned to match that day's transit time (when the moon reaches its highest point). New moon is centered, full moon is at the edge (= midnight transit), first quarter is toward the right (evening), last quarter is toward the left (early morning)
• Daytime moon fades into blue sky — moon marker transparency syncs with the night overlay. Moons that transit during full night (like full moon) appear vivid; moons that transit during day (new moon, or some quarter phases) appear faint
• Expanded moon phase names — displays traditional Japanese lunar names including new moon, crescent, first quarter, thirteenth night, small full, full moon, sixteenth night, waiting moon phases, last quarter, twenty-third night, twenty-sixth night, brightness moon, thirtieth night, and more
• Moon age now based on transit time — the age shown on the chart (days elapsed since the new moon at the moment the moon transits) may differ by half a day from the standard "noon moon age" (see the explanation page at /wbgt/ for details)
• "Astronomical Data" section — now always shown as a collapsible panel at the bottom of the weather card (remembers open/closed state). Includes moon age, moonrise/transit/moonset, brightness, distance, supermoon indicator; sunrise/transit/sunset, three twilight types (astronomical/nautical/civil), and day length
• /wbgt/ page expanded to "How to Read the Weather Chart" — explains each element (background color / temperature / rainfall / night / moon marker / moon age) and links to external resources (Ministry of the Environment heat index, Japan Meteorological Agency, National Astronomical Observatory of Japan)

v1.36.0

We've redesigned the rainfall bar. Now you can see at a glance both the "probability" and "amount" of rain on the same graph. We also made the garbage town selector easier to use.

• Rainfall bar redesigned as "probability + amount" dual-axis display — blue/yellow gradient background shows "when rain is likely", bar height shows "how much will fall"
• 6-hour rainfall probability shown above — hourly probabilities appear in 6-hour blocks, with · marks at block boundaries
• Heavy rain breaks through chart top as spikes — rainfall over 30mm/hour (alert-level) is instantly recognizable
• Rainfall bar and heat index bar heights unified — easier to compare side by side
• Rainfall bar also has nighttime overlay — areas before sunrise and after sunset shown dimly (same as heat index bar)
• Rainfall probability displayed up to one week ahead — now incorporates Japan Meteorological Agency weekly forecasts (previously only today + 1.5 days)
• Days with no rain hide the chart — displays only "🌧 No rain" text to keep the screen clean
• Garbage town selector now concentrates selection — tapping a kana badge hides other kana and displays only that kana's town names in a single row. Swipe left/right to reach a town name. Tap again to restore full view
• Kana badges enlarged — easier to tap, same size as town names for better readability
• "Don't select" button — option when you don't want to see the garbage section. Clearer than the old "hide" option
• Footer operator link corrected to Office TECOLI business page
